summary: AICore device code does not provide scalar float math calls like expf/log1pf/tanhf inside custom kernels. When a fused kernel needs Mish-like nonlinearities, replace direct libc math calls with AscendC-supported operations or a pure-float polynomial/piecewise approximation implemented in kernel code.

error:
op_kernel/conv2d_subtract_hard_swish_max_pool_mish_custom_topqcweymhxtrzqm20260310143742.cpp:131:42: error: double precision operation is not allowed in aicore function
op_kernel/conv2d_subtract_hard_swish_max_pool_mish_custom_topqcweymhxtrzqm20260310143742.cpp:129:51: error: cast to/from double precision floating variable is not allowed in aicore function
summary: Trying to bypass missing float math by casting to double also fails on AICore, because double-precision arithmetic and float-double casts are disallowed in device functions. Keep nonlinear helper code entirely in float and redesign it with piecewise or polynomial approximations over the actual value range instead of introducing double math.
